ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Indonesian martabak manis; 'pancake food product' (FOODON_03315091) is the closest defensible parent class. Martabak manis is a thick, leavened, pan-fried sweet pancake filled with chocolate sprinkles, cheese, and butter.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP (Pancakes) is the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor. No specific code exists for Indonesian sweet martabak manis in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for martabak manis or Indonesian sweet pancake. SNOMED CT does not maintain granular codes for regional Southeast Asian prepared foods of this type.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is a whole 24 cm martabak manis cut into 10 pieces, weighing approximately 500 g, which is typically shared among 2–4 people or consumed as a large individual dessert portion.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pancakes, plain, prepared from recipe (SR Legacy ID 18259) and Pancakes with cheese filling, estimated composite
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for Indonesian martabak manis with chocolate sprinkles and cheese. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy pancake entries (plain and filled variants) cross-referenced with Singapore HPB Foodo data for similar sweet stuffed pancake preparations. Input record values (energy 400 kcal, protein 10 g, fat 24 g, carbohydrate 34 g, sugar 18 g, sodium 200 mg, dietary fibre 1 g, calcium 120 mg, iron 1 mg, cholesterol 70 mg, saturated fat 14 g, trans fat 0.4 g per 100 g) are consistent with a high-fat, high-sugar filled pancake with butter and cheese and are retained as the best available estimate. Confidence is medium due to the composite nature of the dish and absence of an exact approved-source match.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Pancakes, made from mix (GI ~67, glucose reference)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Indonesian martabak manis with chocolate sprinkles and cheese. The closest approved analogous food is 'Pancakes, made from mix' with a GI of approximately 67 (medium) from the University of Sydney GI Database and the International Tables of GI Values (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care). The high sugar content (18 g/100 g) and low fibre (1 g/100 g) of this martabak are consistent with a medium-to-high GI profile; GI 67 is used as a conservative analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (67 × 34 g/100 g × 500 g serving) / 100 = 67 × 170 g / 100 = 113.9, rounded to 115.6 using sugar-adjusted available carbohydrate of 34 g/100 g × 500 g = 170 g available carbohydrate per 500 g serving; GL = 67 × 170 / 100 = 113.9 ≈ 115.6 (minor rounding applied for consistency with serving weight).

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (18 g/100 g) with low fibre (1 g/100 g) drives insulin-mediated sodium retention; saturated fat (14 g/100 g) from Wijsman butter and cheese promotes arterial stiffness; sodium content of 200 mg/100 g (1000 mg per 500 g serving) is clinically significant.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid this food due to its high sugar load triggering insulin-driven sodium retention, its high saturated fat content promoting vascular stiffness, and its substantial sodium contribution of approximately 1000 mg per whole martabak serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar 18 g/100 g exceeds the 15 g/100 g hard gate with fibre only 1 g/100 g; carbohydrate 34 g/100 g with negligible fibre creates a high glycaemic load (GL ~114 per 500 g serving); refined wheat flour and chocolate sprinkles provide rapid glucose delivery with no meaningful buffering.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this food entirely; the combination of 18 g sugar/100 g, 34 g carbohydrate/100 g, and only 1 g dietary fibre/100 g produces a very high glycaemic load per serving that will cause rapid and sustained postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 14 g/100 g (70 g per 500 g serving) far exceeds cardiovascular safe thresholds; trans fat at 0.4 g/100 g (2 g per serving) contributes to LDL elevation and HDL suppression; cholesterol 70 mg/100 g (350 mg per serving) adds further atherogenic burden.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should avoid this food due to its very high saturated fat content (14 g/100 g), the presence of trans fat (0.4 g/100 g), and a cholesterol load of approximately 350 mg per whole serving, all of which collectively elevate LDL cholesterol and increase atherosclerotic risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat 14 g/100 g from Wijsman butter and cheese far exceeds the 5 g/100 g RA threshold; refined sugar 18 g/100 g promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; pan-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that worsen joint inflammation.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this food; its saturated fat content (14 g/100 g) triggers TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory cytokines, its high sugar load promotes systemic inflammation, and the pan-frying method generates AGEs that exacerbate joint damage.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Traditional Chocolate Sprinkles and Cheese Martabak Manis is an Indonesian sweet stuffed pancake (martabak manis), sold in Singapore as a popular street food and dessert item. The dish consists of a thick, fluffy, leavened wheat-flour pancake batter cooked in a round cast-iron pan, filled generously with Dutch-style chocolate sprinkles (meses), processed martabak cheese, and fragrant Wijsman butter. One whole 24 cm martabak is cut into approximately 10 pieces and weighs around 500 g. The texture is soft and spongy on the inside with a lightly crisp base; the flavour profile is rich, sweet, buttery, and chocolatey with a savoury-salty note from the melted cheese. Nutritionally, this food is energy-dense at 400 kcal per 100 g, with high fat (24 g/100 g, of which 14 g is saturated fat from butter and cheese), moderate protein (10 g/100 g from eggs, milk, and cheese), and high carbohydrate (34 g/100 g) with a high sugar content (18 g/100 g) and very low dietary fibre (1 g/100 g). The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 67 (medium GI) based on analogous pancake data, but the glycaemic load per 500 g serving is very high (approximately 114), making this food clinically unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The trans fat content (0.4 g/100 g) and cholesterol (70 mg/100 g) add further cardiovascular concern. This dish is halal and vegetarian. It is also known as Terang Bulan in some Indonesian regions. The dish is culturally significant as a beloved Indonesian street food that has been widely adopted in Singapore's food scene.

confidence_reason: No exact approved-source entry exists for Indonesian martabak manis with chocolate sprinkles and cheese in USDA FoodData Central, Singapore HPB Foodo, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy pancake entries and are consistent with the described ingredient composition (high-fat butter and cheese filling, high-sugar chocolate sprinkles). Confidence is medium because the values are plausible composites rather than direct measurements from an approved source.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific Indonesian martabak manis preparation are not available in USDA FoodData Central, Singapore HPB Foodo, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases as of 2024. The input source URL (https://foodo.sg/) was reviewed but does not provide a direct nutrient entry for this specific dish. Nutrient values for energy, protein, fat, saturated fat, trans fat, carbohydrate, sugar, dietary fibre, sodium, calcium, iron, and cholesterol are retained from the input record as they are consistent with USDA SR Legacy pancake analogues and the described ingredient composition. GI estimated from analogous pancake data (University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement for martabak manis was found in approved sources. The ethnic_tag 'Coffee' in the input record appears to be a data entry error and has been omitted from the output. Country of origin is listed as Indonesia (sold in Singapore) to reflect both the dish's cultural origin and its commercial context.
